DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Richard Zuromski 415-989-5900 of Carroll, Burdick & Mcdonough LLP appearing via Zoom for plaintiff.
Angelita Louie 415-971-4548 defendant in pro per.
No other appearances.

Hearing required and held. Plaintiff states to the court that order granting attorney's motion to be relieved as counsel has been filed but not served to opposing counsel. Discussions regarding her loans, accounts and personal shopping at Gucci and Prada were held. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

Motion to compel is GRANTED-the court does not find that plaintiff Angelita Louie met her burden to establish indigency. Plaintiff Zen Louie, LLC is dismissed.
Demurrer is moot in light of ruling on Motion to Compel Arbitration. Ms. Chau is still considered attorney of record until order served and proof of service on file.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young; Not Reported. (D501)

Discussions regarding her loans, accounts and personal shopping at Gucci and Prada were held. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

Motion to compel is GRANTED-the court does not find that plaintiff Angelita Louie met her burden to establish indigency. Plaintiff Zen Louie, LLC is dismissed.
Demurrer is moot in light of ruling on Motion to Compel Arbitration. Ms. Chau is still considered attorney of record until order served and proof of service on file.

MINI MINUTES: WENDY H. CHAU'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF ZEN LOUIE LLC AND ANGELITA LOUIE

Appearances via Zoom: Wendy H. Chau of the Law Offices of Wendy H. Chau (415.539.6116), counsel for defendants and moving to be relieved; Richard Zuromski of Seifert Zuromski LLP (415.293.7966) for plaintiff and responding party

Ms. Chau informs the Court that defendant Angelita Louie is having connection issues to join the hearing via Zoom.

The motion is discussed and the Court grants the motion. Ms. Chau is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

A hearing concerning Plaintiffs inability to pay for arbitration is set for June 14, 2023. (Aronow v. Superior Court (2022) 76 Cal.App.5th 865.) Plaintiff to file an opening brief regarding insolvency and admissible evidence regarding ability/inability to pay by May 3, 2023. Opposition due May 24, 2023. Reply due May 31, 2023.
